output:
**Yes**, — arriving to school on time is more likely than arriving late.

**Calculation using the law of total probability:**

P(on time) = P(on time | wake on time) × P(wake on time) + P(on time | wake late) × P(wake late)

= 0.52 × 0.95 + 0.87 × 0.05

= 0.494 + 0.0435

= **0.5375 (53.75%)**

Since 53.75% > 50%, arriving on time is more likely than arriving late overall.
